# Implementation Plan: Pokemon Collection Organizer

**Branch**: `001-pokemon-collection` | **Date**: 2025-11-29 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-pokemon-collection/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Build a React web application using Vite that allows users to organize their Pokemon collection by marking Pokemon as collected or adding them to a wishlist. The application will load Pokemon data from a public API, display Pokemon images, and persist user collection state in browser local storage. MVP focuses on core collection tracking (P1 user story) with minimal dependencies.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: JavaScript (ES2020+) with Node.js 18+  
**Primary Dependencies**: React 18, Vite, axios (for API calls), pnpm (build tool)  
**Storage**: Browser localStorage (client-side only, no backend)
**Testing**: Vitest, React Testing Library, pnpm for dependency management  
**Target Platform**: Web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge - modern standards)
**Project Type**: Single-page web application (frontend only)  
**Performance Goals**: <1 second initial load, <100ms API response display, 60 fps interactions  
**Constraints**: Minimal dependencies (React + Vite core only), offline-capable data persistence, public API free tier constraints  
**Scale/Scope**: Single-user local collection, support ~1000 Pokemon entries from public API

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

✅ **Code Quality First**: Single Vite project with clear separation (components, services, hooks, models). No architectural shortcuts. Linting enforced pre-commit.

✅ **Testing Standards (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**: TDD enforced throughout. Unit tests for models, services, and hooks; Integration tests for user flows. Vitest + React Testing Library required.

✅ **User Experience Consistency**: Minimal styling approach with consistent component patterns. Reusable Pokemon card, collection list, and wishlist list components. Clear visual differentiation (badges) between collected and wishlist items.

✅ **Fast Development Velocity**: Vite provides <1s build times. Minimal dependencies reduce complexity and build size. localStorage persistence eliminates backend requirements. Development environment setup documented in quickstart.md.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/[###-feature]/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command - NOT created by /speckit.plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
# React + Vite Single-Page Application
src/
├── components/
│   ├── PokemonCard.jsx          # Individual Pokemon display with collect/wishlist buttons
│   ├── CollectionList.jsx       # Display list of collected Pokemon
│   ├── WishlistList.jsx         # Display list of wishlist Pokemon
│   ├── PokemonSearch.jsx        # Search/filter input for Pokemon index
│   └── App.jsx                  # Main app layout and state management
│
├── services/
│   ├── pokemonApi.js            # API calls to public Pokemon API (PokeAPI)
│   ├── collectionStorage.js     # localStorage management for collection/wishlist persistence
│   └── pokemonService.js        # Business logic for managing collection state
│
├── hooks/
│   ├── useCollection.js         # Custom hook for collection state (collected & wishlist)
│   └── usePokemonApi.js         # Custom hook for Pokemon API calls
│
├── models/
│   ├── Pokemon.js               # Pokemon entity with index, name, image, collected, wishlist flags
│   └── Collection.js            # Collection/Wishlist container model
│
├── styles/
│   ├── App.css                  # Main application styling (minimal approach)
│   └── components.css           # Component-specific styles
│
├── utils/
│   └── constants.js             # API URLs, storage keys, constants
│
└── main.jsx                     # Vite entry point

tests/
├── unit/
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── collectionStorage.test.js
│   │   └── pokemonService.test.js
│   ├── hooks/
│   │   └── useCollection.test.js
│   └── models/
│       └── Pokemon.test.js
│
├── integration/
│   └── collection.flow.test.jsx # Full flow: search → collect → view collection
│
└── contract/
    └── pokemonApi.test.js       # API contract tests (response format validation)

index.html                        # Vite entry HTML
vite.config.js                    # Vite configuration
package.json                      # Package metadata
pnpm-lock.yaml                    # pnpm dependency lock file
.eslintrc.json                    # Linting configuration
.gitignore                        # Git ignore rules
```

**Structure Decision**: Single web application project selected. React + Vite provides fast development with minimal overhead. Component-based architecture supports maintainability and testing. Services layer separates API/storage concerns from UI. No backend required—localStorage provides persistence. All code follows Code Quality First principle with pre-commit linting and TDD enforcement.

## Complexity Tracking

✅ **No Constitution violations detected.** Single-page React application with component-based architecture is the simplest approach for this requirement. All principles are met without justified trade-offs.
